images.caption: Figure 1:Shortcut risk in vanilla OPSD for MLLMs. The student only sees the imageIIand questionxx, but the teacher is also conditioned on the reference answera⋆a^{\star}. Because MLLMs can be strongly influenced by text and may underuse visual input, the known answer can shape reasoning and the final answer before visual evidence is clearly used. The student may then produce an answer-compatible rationale with weak visual grounding.

Figure 2:PALR diagnostic results on Qwen2.5-VL. All numbers are percentages (%).𝒯d\mathcal{T}_{d}is the visual description segment introduced by ViGOS,𝒯r​a\mathcal{T}_{ra}is the merged reasoning-answer segment used in this diagnostic, and𝒯y\mathcal{T}_{y}is the full rollout. A lower PALR indicates less answer-driven supervision under this diagnostic.

Figure 3:Training pipeline of ViGOS. Given an imageIIand questionxx, the student samples a structured trajectoryy=(d,r,a)y=(d,r,a), whereddis a visual description,rris the reasoning process, andaais the final answer. A parser extracts token masks𝒯d\mathcal{T}_{d},𝒯r\mathcal{T}_{r}, and𝒯a\mathcal{T}_{a}and detects invalid rollouts. For valid rollouts, an image-only perception teacher supervisesdd, and a privileged reasoning teacher supervises(r,a)(r,a)on the same student prefix. For invalid rollouts, a reference teacher provides a fallback signal. All teachers are removed at inference time.

Figure 4:Step-wise comparison between OPSD and ViGOS on ViLP.
Prior measures accuracy on prior-aligned questions, while Score measures accuracy on image-supported questions.

Figure A.I:Token-level PALR shift example on Qwen2.5-VL-7B. Blue tokens are more image-driven under ViGOS, and red tokens are more answer-driven under ViGOS. The example shows that ViGOS grounds the visible table entries before reasoning, while still using answer guidance for the computation and final answer.
